title: Adding Three Numbers by Making Ten: 6 + 4 + 7

steps.subgoalLabel: Look for a pair that makes 10
steps.instruction: Check if any two of the three numbers add up to 10.
steps.explanation: Finding pairs that make 10 makes adding easier.

steps.subgoalLabel: Add the ten and the third number
steps.instruction: Now add 10 and the remaining number, 7.
steps.explanation: Adding to 10 is quick because you just put the number after 1.
You can add numbers in any order. Picking pairs that make 10 is a smart strategy!

steps.instruction: Do any two numbers form a doubles fact?
steps.explanation: Doubles facts are quick to recall and give you a nice starting point.

steps.instruction: Add the remaining number to the doubles sum.
steps.explanation: After using the doubles fact, just add what is left.
You can use doubles or make-ten — whichever you spot first!
